mnemonics: JVM Starts Main Thread Automatically (Just Very Magical Start)
keyPoints: Every Java application begins execution with the main thread.
The main thread is created automatically when the program starts and ends when the main() method concludes.
The main thread is the parent thread from which all other 'child' threads are spawned.
commonMistakes: Assuming the main thread is not a 'real' thread and ignoring its thread properties (e.g., priority, name).
Attempting to start the main thread explicitly using the start() method, which is unnecessary and will cause an error.
